title: Windows child-containment path (windows-supervisor.ts + Job Object guard in process-tree.ts) collapses every failure mode to a bare boolean with no captured reason
evidence: windows-supervisor.ts:172-174 wraps the entire launch sequence (parseLaunch, spawn, pipe wiring, pid validation) in one try/catch; any failure -- malformed launch record, protocol overflow (MAX_LAUNCH_BYTES/MAX_CONTROL_BYTES), spawn error, missing stdio pipes, invalid pid -- is swallowed and replaced with the single fixed string 'REFUSED\n' on the status pipe (or nothing at all if even that write throws, per the empty catch). process-tree.ts:520-539 (readStarted) mirrors this on the parent side: a timeout, a malformed 'STARTED n' line, or a stream error all resolve to the same `undefined`, indistinguishable from one another. Likewise startWindowsJobGuard's `ready` promise (process-tree.ts:441-457) resolves to a bare `false` on stderr overflow, helper close, helper error, or a wrong readiness line -- again no captured reason -- and finishOutput()/settleExitCode() discard the Job Object helper's stderr content outright (only byte-counted for overflow, per bridge.ts/runner.ts's established pattern in this same codebase).
impact: On Windows -- one of the three CI matrix OSes for ca-pi per inventory.md -- an operator whose Pi child fails to launch or whose Job Object containment fails to arm gets no diagnosable signal distinguishing a malformed launch record from a spawn failure from a protocol-overflow from a timeout; every path surfaces identically as childFailure()'s generic 'Pi child isolation failed safely ... run /ca-doctor' with nothing written to any log. This is the single most Windows-specific new code path in the branch and has no companion audit trail at all, compounding observability-001.
recommendation: Have windows-supervisor.ts write a minimal reason token (not raw exception text, to respect the redaction contract) alongside REFUSED on the status pipe, and have the parent (process-tree.ts) surface/log that reason distinctly from a plain timeout; extend startWindowsJobGuard's failure paths similarly with a bounded reason code rather than a bare boolean.
acceptance_criteria: windows-supervisor.ts's failure path emits a bounded, redaction-safe reason alongside REFUSED distinguishing at least: invalid launch record, spawn failure, pipe unavailable, invalid pid
process-tree.ts's readStarted()/startWindowsJobGuard() surface that reason (or a locally-determined timeout/overflow reason) to the caller instead of collapsing to undefined/false
the reason is available to doctor.ts or the gate-events.log audit line added under observability-001
